zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Jane Brereton於1370年2月10日出生在英國爾時，她的父親Roger Brereton30歲，母親Ellen28歲。她於1396年與George Eyton在英國爾馬爾帕斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1453年在英國英格蘭史洛普爾艾頓 亞爾 維姆享年83歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Jane Brereton于1370年2月10日出生在英国爾时，她的父亲Roger Brereton30岁，母亲Ellen28岁。她于1396年与George Eyton在英国爾馬爾帕斯结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1453年在英国英格兰史洛普爾艾頓 亞爾 維姆享年83岁。

nameMeanings.name: Brereton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places called Brereton, in Cheshire and Staffordshire, and sometimes from Brearton in Yorkshire or Brierton in Durham. Brereton in Staffordshire is named with Old English brǣr, brēr ‘briar’ + Old English dūn ‘hill’ as its final element; the others are named with Old English brǣr, brēr ‘briar’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Brewerton
meanings: English: variant of   Brereton  , the result of confusion by folk etymology with the occupational name   Brewer  .

variants.name: Brierton
meanings: English: habitational name from Brierton in County Durham (formerly in Yorkshire) or Brearton in North Yorkshire, which are both named with Old English brēr ‘briar’ + tūn ‘farmstead’, or Brereton in Cheshire, which has the same origin (see Brereton ).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Jane Brereton was born on 10 February 1370, in Cheshire, England, her father, Roger Brereton, was 30 and her mother, Ellen, was 28. She married George Eyton in 1396, in Malpas, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1453, in Eyton near Wem, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 83.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Jane Brereton(이)가 1370년 2월 10일 영국 체실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger Brereton(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ellen(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1396년 영국 체실르 맬파스에서 George Eyton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1453년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 이턴 니아어 w에서 83세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com George Eyton em 1396, em Malpas, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Jane Brereton родилась 10 февраля 1370 г. в местности чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Roger Brereton было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Ellen, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени George Eyton в 1396 г. в местности малпас, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1453 г. в местности эитон нир вем, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 83 лет.

Se casó con George Eyton en 1396, en Malpas, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1453, en Eyton near Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 83 años.
